evidence: Global overlay layering relies on scattered literal z-index contracts across modules: `z-[100000]` and comments about `z-[100001]` in `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/components/LightboxShell.tsx`, `z-[100002]` in `src/shared/components/ui/runtime/sonner.tsx`, and explicit `zIndex={100}` usage in `src/pages/Home/components/panes/CreativePartnerPane.tsx`.

Feature hooks couple directly to high-fanout hub facades instead of narrower local contracts: `src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts` imports `Shot` from `src/domains/generation/types/public.ts` and error APIs from `src/shared/lib/errorHandling/runtimeError.ts`.

`src/shared/lib/errorHandling/runtimeError.ts` acts as a shared hub that re-exports multiple behaviors (`normalizeAndPresentError`, `normalizeAndPresentAndRethrow`) plus an alias wrapper (`reportRuntimeError`), increasing change blast radius for low-level error policy changes.
cross_module_architecture.issues_preventing_higher_score: Cross-module UI layering and error/type hub coupling are still encoded through shared implicit contracts and broad facades rather than explicit boundary-specific interfaces.

evidence: Lightbox and inline-edit seams pass very large cross-layer option bags instead of narrow boundary contracts: `useImageLightboxSharedState` forwards many unrelated concerns into `useSharedLightboxState` (src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/ImageLightbox.tsx:229-279), and `useInlineEditState` forwards broad environment/persistence objects into multiple feature hooks (src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts:123-223).

Canonical and legacy task creation flows are mixed at integration boundaries: compat DTOs include both modern and legacy fields, then convert inline (`toCanonicalJoinClipsTaskInput`) while canonical task creation still carries mixed-mode checks (src/shared/lib/tasks/legacy/testing/joinClipsCompatAdapter.ts:32-349, src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts:198-263).

Travel payload composition also accepts legacy compatibility input in the same builder used for canonical flow, increasing boundary ambiguity (src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/payloadBuilder.ts:188-319).

JSX-producing `useMemo` blocks have very large dependency sets, making local logic brittle and hard to reason about: `useVideoLightboxControlsPanel` and render-model memos include dozens of dependencies and deeply nested object reads (src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx:38-141, 178-269).

State assembly functions build very wide return objects with many pass-through fields, increasing internal indirection and change surface (`buildInlineEditStateModels`) (src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts:260-320).

Task param parsing relies on long key-fallback ladders across many shapes (`deriveInputImages`, `derivePrompt`), which indicates low local precision and difficult-to-maintain branching (src/shared/lib/taskParamsUtils.ts:34-152).

`TravelBetweenImagesTaskParams` combines modern API fields, legacy migration fields, UI-only metadata-bearing variants, and worker orchestration controls in one wide contract (`src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/types.ts`).

`buildTravelRequestBodyV2` conditionally omits semantically important arrays (`image_generation_ids`, `image_variant_ids`, `pair_shot_generation_ids`) when lengths drift, instead of failing contract validation (`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/services/generateVideoService.ts`).
`useLineageChain` surfaces a mixed error API (custom `LineageScopeError` plus normalized runtime errors), while `generationTaskRepository` exposes status-object contracts for similar scope checks.

Multiple files are now compatibility-only re-export shells with migration prose but no domain behavior (`src/tools/image-generation/components/ImageGenerationForm/index.tsx`, `src/tools/image-generation/components/ImageGenerationForm/types.ts`, `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/PhaseConfigVertical.tsx`, `src/tools/join-clips/settings.ts`).

Several migration shims duplicate deprecation metadata and ceremony (owner/remove-by/remediation wrappers) rather than converging on one retireable boundary (`src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts`, `src/shared/lib/toolConstants.ts`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/legacyStructureVideo.ts`).
`src/shared/types/referenceImage.ts` retains broad legacy fields with open-ended removal wording ('will be removed after bulk migration'), extending mixed-shape payload debt.

findings.summary: UI layering depends on hardcoded z-index values spread across unrelated modules
evidence: `LightboxShell` hardcodes `z-[100000]` and documents dependency on another component at `z-[100001]` in comments, creating an implicit cross-module stack contract.
`Toaster` uses `z-[100002]` independently in `runtime/sonner.tsx`, extending the same stack with no shared token source.
`CreativePartnerPane` passes `zIndex={100}` to `GlassSidePane`, showing additional ad-hoc layering outside a centralized policy.
findings.suggestion: Create a shared layering contract (e.g., `zLayers.ts` design tokens or a `LayerManager`) and replace literal z-index values/comments with imported constants so cross-module ordering is explicit and versionable.

findings.summary: Media gallery hooks depend on broad shared facades instead of boundary-scoped contracts
evidence: `useMediaGalleryHandlers` imports `Shot` from domain-wide barrel `types/public.ts` (high fanout), coupling gallery behavior to the full shared type surface.
The same hook also imports global error presentation/rethrow APIs from `runtimeError.ts`, binding feature-level control flow to shared runtime policy.
Inside the hook, only narrow shot fields are actually used for navigation/lookup, but dependency is on the broad shared domain export surface.
findings.suggestion: Introduce gallery-local boundary contracts (e.g., `NavigableShot` and a feature error port) and map from domain/runtime modules at composition boundaries, so MediaGallery no longer directly depends on high-fanout global facades.

findings.summary: Runtime error facade exposes overlapping entrypoints that widen coupling and change blast radius
evidence: `runtimeError.ts` re-exports two behaviors and adds `reportRuntimeError` wrapper that simply delegates to `normalizeAndPresentError`, expanding a heavily shared API surface.
Feature hooks consume the facade directly (`useMediaGalleryHandlers`, `useMediaGalleryItemState`), so even small policy/API changes in the facade affect many modules.
The wrapper/alias style encourages importing from the central hub instead of using narrower behavior-specific modules.
findings.suggestion: Slim the shared runtime error surface to minimal canonical exports, deprecate redundant aliases, and have feature modules depend on explicit error strategy interfaces injected at their boundary.

findings.summary: Integration seams rely on oversized cross-layer option bags instead of narrow contracts.
evidence: `useSharedLightboxState` is invoked with a broad object that mixes navigation, shot workflow, edit mode flags, download/delete controls, and layout concerns in one boundary call (src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/ImageLightbox.tsx:229-279).

`useInlineEditState` composes `env` and `persistence` aggregates, then threads many fields through multiple feature hooks (`useInpainting`, `useMagicEditMode`, `useRepositionMode`, `useImg2ImgMode`) (src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts:123-223).

`useVideoLightboxRenderModel` similarly composes multi-domain models (variants/navigation/edit/panel state) into one render model object, requiring large coordination across collaborators (src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx:154-320).
findings.suggestion: Define small seam contracts per concern (navigation, variants, edit actions, panel data) and add adapter functions at one boundary layer. Keep downstream hooks/components dependent on only one concern-specific interface each.

findings.summary: Large JSX `useMemo` dependency arrays create brittle local logic and high indirection cost.
evidence: `useVideoLightboxControlsPanel` memoizes JSX with a very large dependency list tied to deep nested fields, which is difficult to validate and maintain safely (src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx:38-141).

`lightboxVariants` and `lightboxStateValue` memos repeat the same wide dependency pattern, indicating local complexity from object-graph threading rather than focused rendering logic (src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx:178-269).

`buildInlineEditStateModels` produces broad assembled state objects with many pass-through methods, adding internal indirection before rendering consumers use them (src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts:260-320).
findings.suggestion: Replace JSX-in-`useMemo` patterns with smaller presentational components and pass stable primitive props. Use targeted memoization only around expensive computations, not full UI trees.

findings.summary: Legacy compatibility logic remains embedded in canonical task seams, blurring handoff contracts.
evidence: `JoinClipsTaskCompatParams` contains both canonical and legacy fields, and conversion logic resolves multiple source modes in one path (`clip_source`, `clips`, legacy paths, legacy video edit fields) (src/shared/lib/tasks/legacy/testing/joinClipsCompatAdapter.ts:32-349).

Canonical join-clips normalization still carries several mixed-mode assertions and mode checks (`video_edit` rules + clip source kind rules), showing seam complexity from overlap (src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts:227-263).

Travel payload builder includes explicit `legacyStructureCompatibility` handling and legacy field wiring in canonical payload composition (src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/payloadBuilder.ts:188-314).
findings.suggestion: Move legacy support behind isolated migration entrypoints and keep canonical builders legacy-free. Canonical modules should accept only canonical DTOs and reject migration logic at compile-time.

findings.summary: Task param readers use long fallback ladders that encode multiple implicit schemas locally.
evidence: `deriveInputImages` checks many competing keys (`image_url`, `image`, `input_image`, `init_image`, `orchestrator_details`, `full_orchestrator_payload`, segment-specific fields) in one function (src/shared/lib/taskParamsUtils.ts:34-85).
`derivePrompt` similarly mixes segment/orchestrator/top-level prompt sources with many fallbacks, including enhanced/base variants (src/shared/lib/taskParamsUtils.ts:101-152).

Task builders emit multiple payload shapes across flows (orchestrator details, family payload, segment params), driving these reader heuristics instead of a stable read contract (src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/payloadBuilder.ts:241-388, src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts:305-360).
findings.suggestion: Introduce explicit discriminated read contracts per task family and parse once into typed view models. Replace key-scanning heuristics with family-specific extractors selected by contract version/type.

findings.summary: Travel task API type mixes backend, migration, and UI concerns into one unstable surface
evidence: `TravelBetweenImagesTaskParams` extends multiple partial interfaces and also includes legacy, migration, orchestration, and UI-restoration fields in one interface.
`StructureVideoConfigWithMetadata` carries UI-only fields (`metadata`, `resource_id`) in the same type family as request payload contracts.
`generateVideoService` builds request bodies against this broad type, making it easy to leak non-API concerns across the boundary.
findings.suggestion: Split the contract into explicit boundary DTOs: `TravelUiState`, `TravelRequestPayload`, and `TravelLegacyInput`. Keep conversion in one adapter module and have `createTravelBetweenImagesTask` accept only the strict request payload type.

findings.summary: Lineage scope API returns status objects in one layer but throws heterogeneous errors in another
evidence: `resolveVariantProjectScope` returns a structured status union (`ok`, `scope_mismatch`, `query_failed`, etc.).
`fetchVariantById` converts some scope states into `LineageScopeError` but DB read failures into `normalizeAndPresentError(...)` throws, producing mixed runtime error types for callers.
`useLineageChain` then exposes `error: Error | null`, losing typed discriminants from the repository contract.
findings.suggestion: Unify to one error/result contract across the seam (prefer a discriminated `Result` object) and keep hook return typing aligned with that contract so consumers can branch predictably without instanceof checks.

findings.summary: Generation-task mapping shape is duplicated across cache and repository layers
evidence: `GenerationTaskMapping` and local `GenerationTaskCacheEntry` both represent `{ taskId, status, queryError }` with separate definitions.
`preloadGenerationTaskMappings` re-maps repository output into a parallel cache type instead of reusing one canonical boundary type.
Any future status/code change requires synchronized edits in both files, increasing seam drift risk.
findings.suggestion: Export and reuse a single mapping DTO type (or shared serializer) from repository to cache layer, and store that canonical type directly in query cache.

findings.summary: Travel payload assembly hides pair-mapping inconsistencies by dropping fields
evidence: `buildTravelRequestBodyV2` only includes `image_generation_ids`, `image_variant_ids`, and `pair_shot_generation_ids` when lengths exactly match expected counts; otherwise it silently omits them.
These arrays are generated from separately filtered data paths (`buildImagePayload`, pair derivations), so drift can happen without a hard failure.
Type comments in task types indicate these IDs are important for lineage/tethering, but current seam degrades silently instead of enforcing consistency.
findings.suggestion: Introduce an explicit payload integrity check before request construction; if counts drift, return a validation failure with diagnostics instead of omitting fields. Centralize image/pair derivation into one immutable intermediate model used by all downstream arrays.

findings.summary: Compatibility-only re-export shells create persistent migration noise with little runtime value
evidence: These modules mostly contain comments about prior moves plus a single re-export statement.
They preserve old import paths but add no behavior, tests, or type constraints, so they prolong duplicate entry points.
The pattern appears in multiple tool areas, indicating migration debt was replicated instead of sunset through one coordinated boundary.
findings.suggestion: Set explicit removal milestones per shim, run an import-path codemod to canonical modules, then delete these wrapper files in one migration PR (with a temporary lint rule that blocks new imports from the legacy paths).

findings.summary: Join-clips compatibility adapter is a large mixed-shape translator that obscures core intent
evidence: `JoinClipsTaskCompatParams` aggregates dozens of optional legacy/canonical fields and mode-specific payload variants in one type.
`toCanonicalJoinClipsTaskInput` performs many boundary checks and strips numerous legacy fields via throwaway destructuring, making changes fragile.
A similar legacy-to-canonical shape bridge exists for travel structure video types, showing the same compatibility pattern repeated across task families.
findings.suggestion: Split compatibility translation into small mode-specific normalizers (clip source, video edit, shared params) with discriminated unions, and keep legacy parsing at a single entry boundary before handing a strict canonical type to downstream code.

findings.summary: Deprecation signaling is hidden behind routine reads/calls, increasing local surprise
evidence: `toolConstants.ts` wraps `TOOL_IDS` and `TOOL_ROUTES` in a Proxy that emits deprecation enforcement on each property access.
`handleError.ts` wrappers trigger alias-usage signaling before delegating to canonical functions, adding side effects to what look like pass-through calls.
This makes call sites pay observability/enforcement behavior implicitly instead of at explicit migration boundaries.
findings.suggestion: Move deprecation reporting to explicit import-time or one-time module initialization gates (or call-site codemod metrics), and keep alias exports as transparent references without per-access runtime hooks.
